title: Delete dead web/src/version.ts, surface the real live backend version in switcher's health-dot tooltip
action: web/src/version.ts's appVersion() is unused (no caller besides its own test) and hardcodes a now-wrong "0.1.0" (Cargo.toml is 0.1.2). Delete web/src/version.ts and web/test/version.test.ts entirely. Then in web/src/views/switcher.ts's loadHealth() function (around line 211-216), which already calls fetchHealth() and receives a HealthInfo with an unused `.version` field, extend the healthDot's title attribute to include the version, e.g. change `healthDot.setAttribute("title", health?.herdr_up ? "herdr is up" : "herdr is unreachable");` to something like `healthDot.setAttribute("title", health ? `herdr-go ${health.version} \u00b7 ${health.herdr_up ? "herdr is up" : "herdr is unreachable"}` : "herdr is unreachable");` (adjust wording/formatting to match the file's existing style - the point is: when health is available, the tooltip shows the real backend version alongside the existing up/down status; when health is null, keep the existing fallback text unchanged).

truths: web/src/version.ts and web/test/version.test.ts no longer exist
the health-dot's title attribute includes the real health.version string when health data is available
the existing up/down status text and health-dot CSS classes (health-up/health-down) are unchanged
no new API call added - health.version was already being fetched and simply wasn't used before
key_links: web/src/api.ts's HealthInfo interface already declares version: string
prohibitions: Do not add a new API endpoint or a new fetch call
Do not change health-dot's up/down CSS class logic

verify: cd web && npx tsc --noEmit && npx vite build && npx vitest --run

trace.outcome: Deleted dead web/src/version.ts (unused appVersion(), hardcoded stale 0.1.0) and its test; switcher.ts's loadHealth() now surfaces the real backend health.version in the health-dot tooltip alongside the existing up/down text
